meaning: adj.狂怒的,狂暴的,激烈的,热烈兴奋的,极为激烈
examples: He risked a glance at her furious face.
The miners were furious and threatened to walk out.
She drove off at a furious pace.
Word of Cabinet appointments by President-elect Barack Obama flew fast and furious late Friday afternoon: Sen.
The still-not-publisher of Half-Life 3 is of course no stranger to furious speculation about its intentions.
